avoid forcing consumers to declare a separate core dependency.

commit.message:
chore: remove legacy mirror-repos.yml workflow

Superseded by sync-to-org.yml which handles both new repos and updates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix BFP8_B packing: tilize matrix before pack_as_bfp_tiles (correct GEMV output)

pack_as_bfp_tiles expects pre-tilized input where each contiguous 1024-element
block forms one 32×32 tile. We were passing raw row-major matrix data, causing
completely wrong weight tile contents and garbage model output.

Fix: reorganize [M,K] row-major bf16 data into tile-major order (each tile is
a contiguous 32×32 row-major block) before calling pack_as_bfp_tiles.

Also: switch weight reads to interleaved DRAM via TensorAccessor (replacing
incorrect DRAM-sharded bank addressing), add matmul transpose flag for correct
GEMV semantics (y = W @ x), and clean up debug prints.

Model now produces correct text: "The capital of France is Paris."

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
CR-9: Remove educational resource and mission paragraph from our-organization page

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Track provider quota from warden, cifix, reviewfix, and schematic sessions (Forge-g5m) (#158)

* fix: track provider quota from warden, cifix, reviewfix, and schematic sessions

Only smith.Spawn was capturing and persisting provider quota (rate-limit
headers) to state.db. The warden, cifix, reviewfix, and schematic workers
all spawn claude sessions but discarded the quota data, causing the
dashboard quota display to undercount actual usage.

- Warden: call db.UpsertProviderQuota after smith result
- CIFix: call p.DB.UpsertProviderQuota after smith result
- ReviewFix: call p.DB.UpsertProviderQuota after smith result
- Schematic: add Quota field to Result, persist in pipeline.go

Bead: Forge-g5m

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-g5m

- cifix/reviewfix: move UpsertProviderQuota inside the provider loop so
  quota is persisted per-attempt (including rate-limited ones), fixing
  the wrong-provider bug when all providers are rate limited
- warden: same fix — upsert quota inside the loop before the early return
  on all-providers-exhausted, removing the stale "claude session" comment
- pipeline_test: add TestSchematic_Quota_PersistedToStateDB asserting that
  schematic quota is written to provider_quotas in state.db

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Robin Smith <Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(hearth): rework Live Activity to use one persistent group per event type

Instead of grouping consecutive same-type events (creating many small
groups), merge ALL entries of the same type into a single persistent
group with an accumulating counter. Think groups expand by default;
all others collapse. Expansion toggling is keyed by event type string
instead of group index.

Fix Gmail 401 errors and VIP warranty detection

- Fix: Update httpx client Authorization header when token refreshes
  This fixes the critical bug where tokens were refreshed but the HTTP
  client continued using expired tokens, causing 401 errors every hour.

- Fix: Include 24-hour warranties as VIP (changed < 24 to <= 24)
  Ensures warranties with czas_naprawy=24 trigger admin alerts.

- Add enhanced logging for token refresh debugging

Files modified:
- src/guarantee_email_agent/agent/runner.py (httpx header update)
- src/guarantee_email_agent/email/__init__.py (enhanced logging)
- instructions/steps/valid-warranty.md (VIP condition update)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Add design-handoff, figma-responsive, and design-critique features

- /design-handoff: Generate developer handoff docs (token maps, spacing specs, component APIs, CSS/Tailwind/TS exports)
- /figma-responsive: Generate mobile (375×812) and tablet (768×1024) variants from desktop Figma frames

New agent:
- design-critique: Automated UX heuristic review using Nielsen's 10 heuristics, visual audit, interaction state checks

Updated plugin.json (v1.1.0), SKILL.md (new commands/roles/examples), and README (agents, file tree, knowledge breakdown).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

phase 3: RBAC hardening, org enrollment restriction, Plus access, pass threshold

RBAC:
- Add server action (admin/users/actions.ts) to set Firebase custom claims
  when admin creates or updates a user's role; role is now embedded in
  the JWT so middleware enforces it correctly on next login
- Custom claims are verified against Firestore role before being set

Enrollment:
- Org-assigned learners can no longer self-enroll; they receive a clear
  message directing them to their administrator
- Paid course enrollment now checks for active Plus subscription or
  completed individual purchase before allowing access
- Payment-required courses show a 'coming soon' toast until bank
  integration is implemented

Plus & Purchases:
- Add Subscription and Purchase types to types.ts
- Replace hardcoded isPlusSubscriber=false with real Firestore checks
  against subscriptions/{uid} and purchases collection
- Enroll button label reflects actual access state (Plus, purchased, paid)

Course form:
- Add passThreshold field (1-100%, default 70) to admin course form;
  value is already read by the exam server action from Phase 2

run_method('react', ...) so green dot and target ring update in real time.

Phase 2: Validation baseline is advisory. Remove baseline_ok from hard
pass/fail gate; add pre-flight ui.dialog warning if vessel_o3_pct > 0.01
at start, with Cancel / Proceed anyway via asyncio.Event.

Phase 3: ±1σ confidence band on Power-O3 curve. PowerO3Model gains
ci_power/ci_sigma fields computed via Jacobian propagation from pcov
(einsum J @ pcov @ J.T). Band rendered as fill='toself' Plotly trace.
load_model() hardened to filter unknown JSON keys. Existing model JSONs
load without ci_power/ci_sigma and get empty defaults (backward compat).

Phase 4: Validation result card title simplified to
"{PASSED|FAILED} — {dev:.1f}% deviation" — removes confusing sample count.

Also includes: 4 LPM model re-fit from 2026-03-09 calibration data,
decisions_log.md and dashboard_agent_summary.md updated for Session 12.

commit.message:
Fix Air Quality / 10-Day bottom alignment via explicit h-full chain

CSS Grid stretches grid items via layout, but does not set their CSS
height property — it stays 'auto'. A child's h-full resolves against
its containing block's CSS height, not the stretched layout height.
Without an explicit height on the wrapper div, WeatherDetailsCards'
h-full computes to 'auto' and the AQ card never fills the row.

Adding lg:h-full to the wrapper div sets height: 100% of the grid
area (definite after intrinsic sizing), giving WeatherDetailsCards a
concrete value to resolve against and completing the chain:

  grid row 3 (definite) → wrapper lg:h-full → WeatherDetailsCards
  h-full → grid-rows-[auto_auto_1fr] → AQ card h-full = 1fr ✓

No circular inflation: during intrinsic sizing, percentage heights
on grid items are treated as 'auto', so the row height is still
determined by natural content before any h-full kicks in.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Web UI (index.html):

  and any window.StacksProvider-compatible wallet

  stx_signStructuredMessage — wallet shows user exactly what they sign

phase 2: server-side auth, middleware, exam scoring, retake cooldown

Security & Auth:
- Add Firebase Admin SDK (src/lib/firebase-admin.ts)
- Add /api/auth/session route: POST creates HttpOnly session cookie from
  Firebase ID token, DELETE clears it on logout
- Add middleware.ts with server-side route protection using JWT decode;
  enforces auth on /dashboard, /learn and role-gating on /admin,
  /instructor, /hr, /organization-manager before any page renders
- Login, signup, and Google auth now create a session cookie on success
- Logout now calls DELETE /api/auth/session to clear the cookie

Exam integrity:
- Move exam scoring entirely to a server action (actions.ts); client
  submits raw answers, server fetches questions and scores server-side
  using Firebase Admin SDK — score can no longer be manipulated client-side
- Enforce 1-hour retake cooldown server-side via lastExamAttemptDate field
- Certificate creation moved to server action with full session verification
- ExamViewer now submits answers instead of a client-computed score

Types:
- Add passThreshold to Course (instructor-configurable, defaults to 70)
- Add lastExamAttemptDate to LearnerCourse for retake enforcement

fix: eliminate synchronous DB calls from main thread (#390)

* fix: eliminate synchronous DB calls from main thread

Convert all getDistinct*() methods to cache-only (return {} on miss,
trigger async fetch via requestDistinctValueAsync()). Add grinder_brand
to requestDistinctCache() pre-warm. Make updateTotalShots() async.
Remove 21 dead synchronous methods with zero callers (~1060 lines).
Flag importShotRecord() main-thread violation as TODO.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review findings for async DB cache safety

- Fix cache poisoning: requestDistinctValueAsync now checks withTempDb
  return value and skips cache insert on DB open failure (allows retry)
- Fix stale data race: clear m_pendingDistinctKeys during full cache
  refresh so in-flight callbacks discard stale results
- Add missing qWarning logging for count query failures in initialize(),
  updateTotalShots(), refreshTotalShots(), and getShotCountStatic()
- Check query.prepare() return value separately from exec()
- Update stale comments referencing removed getShot() method
- Clarify audit doc entries for removed methods and caller descriptions

* fix: clear coffee field when selecting a new roaster

When selecting a roaster from suggestions, the coffee (bean type) field
was not cleared, leaving a mismatched roaster/coffee combination.
Added onSuggestionSelected to clear bean type in all three locations:
BeanInfoPage, BrewDialog, and PostShotReviewPage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: ColoredIcon not rendering in picker dialogs

Material style Button adds default insets that squeezed the icon out of
view when ColoredIcon was placed in a Row with explicit width but no
height. Fix by zeroing all insets/padding and setting display to
IconOnly. Also normalize tick.svg to standard Feather icon format and
add explicit height on picker dialog tick usages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: preserve profile_kb_id on backup restore and remove wasteful delete side-effects

Two fixes from PR review findings:

1. importDatabaseStatic() was missing profile_kb_id in its INSERT,
   silently dropping AI dial-in history data on backup restore/merge.

2. deleteShot() (private, only called from importShotRecord during
   overwrite) was spawning async updateTotalShots threads and cache
   invalidations per deleted shot. These are unnecessary since the
   import batch already calls refreshTotalShots() at completion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

When selecting a roaster from suggestions:
- Clear coffee (bean type) and roast date since they belong to the prior roaster

Applied to BeanInfoPage, BrewDialog, and PostShotReviewPage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

A different coffee implies a different roast batch, so clear the roast
date when a new coffee is selected from suggestions.

feat: arquitetura dois ffmpegs com relay Node.js (zero-disconnect)

ffmpegB (keeper) lê do stdin via pipe do Node.js → encode x264 CBR → RTMP.
ffmpegA (content) e ffmpegFiller (black) escrevem no relay → stdin do ffmpegB.

Handoff seamless filler→content: troca no exato instante do primeiro chunk,
sem gap de dados no RTMP. Quando URL expira, filler assume imediatamente
enquanto yt-dlp busca nova URL — ffmpegB e a conexão RTMP nunca param.

commit.message:
fix: correct runClifford classical feed-forward and add Circuit.random (#2)

* fix: correct runClifford classical feed-forward and add Circuit.random

Three changes:

1. runClifford: fix classical feed-forward (if ops)
   - The condition was never evaluated — inner ops always fired regardless of
     creg value.  Now uses per-shot shotCregs to track mid-circuit measurement
     outcomes and checks the condition before recursing, matching run() semantics.
   - Validation now recurses into if-body ops, so non-Clifford gates inside
     conditional blocks are caught early.
   - Fix final readout: instead of using stale mid-circuit measured[] values,
     always re-sample the post-correction Clifford state (+ apply pMeas SPAM
     noise), matching how run() samples from the final statevector.

2. runClifford: populate cregCounts correctly
   Previously cregCounts was initialised to zeros after the shot loop and
   never filled.  Now accumulated per-shot from shotCregs.

3. Circuit.random(nQubits, nGates, seed?)
   New static factory that generates random circuits for benchmarking,
   quantum-volume estimation, and noise characterisation workflows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: close three review gaps from self-audit

- applyCliffords(op.ops) now calls flattenOps first, so named gate calls
  (subcircuits) inside if bodies are expanded at simulation time, matching
  what validateOps already does at validation time
- add test: non-Clifford gate inside an if body throws at runClifford()
- tighten |+⟩ correction test precision from 1 to 2 decimal places

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
